Title: The genome of giant waterlily provides insights into the origin of angiosperms, leaf gigantism, and stamen function innovation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: protection of reproductive organs from damage by pollinating beetles / defense
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: glycoside hydrolase family 19; class I chitinase
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: one of the top two most highly expressed genes in inner stamens, especially 12-St-5w on first day of blooming
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in inner stamens associated with pink module
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: first day of blooming; inner stamen developmental stage
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; WGCNA coexpression; family annotation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: likely contributes to defense/protection of inner stamens while beetles are trapped in flowers
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Vicchitinase1 was the second most highly expressed gene in inner stamens together with VicSABATHa. 2. It is a class I chitinase and was enriched in the pink module associated with inner stamen development. 3. The authors infer a protective role against damage from pollinating beetles, indicating division of labor in Victoria stamens between attraction and defense.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a direct nutrient metabolite gene, but a core experimentally emphasized gene associated with floral functional innovation and coexpressed with the volatile-biosynthesis gene.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing PRJCA040089; transcriptome RNA-seq in floral tissues

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: short-chain fatty acid methyl esters (including methyl 2-methylbutyrate, methyl tiglate, methyl hexanoate)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: short-chain fatty acid methyl ester branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: short-chain fatty acids including butyric acid, tiglic acid, hexanoic acid; also decanoic acid with lower efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: methyl esters including methyl tiglate and methyl hexanoate; in transient expression also 4-methyl-2-pentenoic acid methyl ester and 2-hydroxy-3-methyl-pentanoic acid methyl ester
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: methylates short-chain fatty acids to produce volatile methyl esters
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme in the final step; pivotal catalytic gene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: specifically and ultra-highly expressed in inner stamens, especially 12-St-5w and first day of blooming
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in inner stamens of V. cruziana on first day of blooming; transient overexpression tested in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: first versus second day of blooming; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: VicmetE; VicSAMS; VicSAHH (upstream pathway genes, co-expressed)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GC-MS floral volatile profiling; WGCNA/coexpression; recombinant protein in vitro methyltransferase assays; enzyme kinetics; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS of emitted volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes biosynthesis and emission of short-chain fatty acid methyl esters in V. cruziana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VicSABATHa was the highest expressed gene in inner stamens and was strongly associated with the pink module linked to inner stamen development. 2. It functions as the key enzyme in the final step of methyl ester biosynthesis in V. cruziana. 3. Recombinant VicSABATHa showed high activity toward butyric, tiglic, and hexanoic acids, with highest catalytic efficiency for tiglic acid. 4. Tobacco leaves overexpressing VicSABATHa released diverse short-chain fatty acid methyl esters, validating its catalytic role in FVOC formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Compared with NcSABATH1, VicSABATHa has evolved higher catalytic activity toward short-chain fatty acids and underlies the species-specific methyl-ester-rich floral scent of Victoria.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing PRJCA040089; transcriptome RNA-seq in floral tissues; GC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: medium-/long-chain fatty acid methyl esters including methyl 6-methyl heptanoate and methyl octanoate; also methyl benzoate reported in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: methyl ester biosynthesis / floral volatile organic compound biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: medium-/long-chain fatty acid methyl ester branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: decanoic acid (capric acid) preferentially; weak activity toward tiglic and hexanoic acids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: methylates fatty acids, preferentially medium-/long-chain substrates, to form volatile methyl esters
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established as rate-limiting; validated catalytic enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in petals of N. colorata
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested by transient overexpression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant protein in vitro methyltransferase assays; enzyme kinetics; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS of emitted volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: contributes to biosynthesis of medium-/long-chain fatty acid methyl esters in N. colorata
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NcSABATH1 preferentially catalyzes long-chain fatty acids such as decanoic acid. 2. Transient expression in tobacco leaves produced methyl 6-methyl heptanoate, methyl octanoate, methyl benzoate, and related compounds. 3. NcSABATH1 differs from VicSABATHa in substrate preference and helps explain scent divergence between N. colorata and V. cruziana.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a comparative focal enzyme to show functional divergence of SABATH genes among waterlilies.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: comparative transcriptome RNA-seq; GC-MS metabolite profiling
